title: Salty Spanish Sea Bass Stir-fry
description: A cozy Spanish dish featuring sea bass fillets, a tadka for added depth of flavor, and packed with nutrients to keep you energized.

ingredients.name: Chili Powder
ingredients.notes: Optional for extra heat, adjust to taste

ingredients.name: Red Chili Flakes
ingredients.notes: For a slight kick without too much spice

ingredients.name: Low-Sodium Soy Sauce or Tamari
ingredients.notes: For brining the fish if desired

instructions: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Season the sea bass fillets with a mixture of low-sodium soy sauce or tamari, salt, and pepper.

While waiting, prepare the tadka base by heating a tablespoon of oil in a pan over medium heat. Add onions and garlic; sauté until translucent and aromatic. Stir in tomato paste, chili powder, and red chili flakes (if using). Simmer for about 1 minute to meld the flavors.

Heat another tablespoon of oil in your wok or large frying pan on high heat. Add sea bass fillets skin-side down; cook for a couple of minutes until golden brown on one side. Flip them and cook for another couple of minutes until cooked through, ensuring they reach an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C). Remove from the pan.

Return wok or frying pan to heat. Add more oil if needed, then place tadka base mixture into the hot pan. Stir in additional low-sodium soy sauce or tamari as desired. Simmer briefly to combine flavors and thicken slightly.

Add sautéed onions and garlic from step 2 into the skillet with tadka. Carefully add cooked sea bass fillets, skin-side up; cook for a minute on each side allowing the fish's natural oils to release, infusing with tadka flavor.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs like parsley or dill if desired.
